records.ety: [L. selectus, p. p. of seligere to select; pref. se- aside + levere to gather. See Legend.]

defs: Taken from a number by preferance; picked out as more valuable or exellent than others; of special value or exellence; nicely chosen; selected; choice.
quotes: A few select spirits had separated from the crowd, and formed a fit audience round a far greater teacher.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Selected; p. pr. & vb. n. Selecting.]

defs: To choose and take from a number; to take by preference from among others; to pick out; to cull; as, to select the best authors for perusal.
quotes: The pious chief . . . A hundred youths from all his train selects.
